query: "Crime and Punishment" 564 pages Pevear Volokhonsky translation
answer: Crime and Punishment is a 564-page novel translated by Richard Pevear and Larissa Volokhonsky. It is a psychological thriller by Fyodor Dostoevsky. The translation is highly acclaimed.

results.snippet: Crime and Punishment: Pevear & Volokhonsky Translation (Vintage Classics) - GOOD - Picture 1 of 1. # Crime and Punishment: Pevear & Volokhonsky Translation (Vintage Classics) - GOOD. Hailed by Washington Post Book World as "the best [translation] currently available" when it was first published, this second edition of Crime and Punishment has been updated in honor of the 200th anniversary of Dostoevsky's birth. Dostoevsky's drama of sin, guilt, and redemption transforms the sordid story of an old woman's murder into the nineteenth century's profoundest and most compelling philosophical novel., Nominated as one of America's best-loved novels by PBS's The Great American Read With the same suppleness, energy, and range of voices that won their translation of The Brothers Karamazov the PEN/Book-of-the-Month Club Prize, Pevear and Volokhonsky offer a brilliant translation of Dostoevsky's classic novel that presents a clear insight into this astounding psychological thriller.
